data.title: Vuzix 10-K: Revenue $6.28M, EPS (0.42) — Annual Results
data.description:
Vuzix reported annual total sales of $6.28 million and a basic and diluted net loss per common share of $(0.42), reflecting an improved but still negative profitability posture versus the prior year. The company narrowed its net loss to $(32.27) million for the period, driven by reduced operating losses and an improved gross result. Management highlights continued momentum in revenue and operational changes supporting future margin improvement.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Sales: $6.28 million, up from $5.75 million in 2024.
  • Gross Loss: $(1.06) million, improved from $(5.60) million in 2024.
  • Loss From Operations: $(32.51) million, improved from $(73.97) million in 2024.
  • Net Loss: $(32.27) million, improved from $(73.54) million in 2024.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss per Common Share: $(0.42), improved from $(1.08) in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth: Year-over-year revenue increase attributed to core product demand and expanded service offerings, with management noting sequential growth momentum.
  • Channel Shifts: Accelerated move toward direct-to-consumer and digital channels, increasing the online sales mix and reducing dependence on third-party retailers.
  • Brand Momentum: Strengthened positioning through new product launches and marketing campaigns, along with improved customer retention metrics.
  • Operational Milestones: Expanded manufacturing capacity and optimized supply chain processes, achieving higher throughput and reduced lead times.
  • Forward Outlook: Management forecasts continued top-line growth and margin improvement supported by channel mix, new products, and operational efficiencies.

Original SEC Filing: Vuzix Corp [ VUZI ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: AudioEye 10-K: Revenue $40.3M, EPS not stated
data.description:
AudioEye reported fiscal-year revenue of $40.311 million, a 15% increase versus 2024, while earnings per share were not stated in the filing. The company narrowed losses, recording an operating loss of $1.83 million (a 46% improvement) and a net loss of $3.077 million (improved 28% year over year). The results reflect growth across both Partner/Marketplace and Enterprise channels and continued investment in product and R&D.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $40.311 million, increased 15% vs. 2024
  • Gross Profit: $31.556 million, increased 13% vs. 2024
  • Operating loss: $(1.830) million, improved (46%) vs. 2024 operating loss
  • Net loss: $(3.077) million, improved 28% vs. 2024 net loss
  • Net Income Per Share: Not stated

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Total revenue rose 15% year over year to $40.3 million, driven by expansion in both the Partner/Marketplace and Enterprise channels.
  • Channel Momentum: Partner & Marketplace grew 10% and Enterprise grew 21%; Partner channel represented approximately 58% of ARR while Enterprise comprised about 42%.
  • Customer Base Expansion: Total customers increased to roughly 131,000 from 127,000, with the Partner channel responsible for the net gain.
  • Product & R&D Focus: Continued investment in product innovation, with total R&D spending around 16% of revenue and an increase in capitalized development costs.
  • Operational Milestone: Annual recurring revenue rose about 9% to approximately $40 million and the company expanded into the European Union, supporting Enterprise customer wins.

Original SEC Filing: AUDIOEYE INC [ AEYE ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Why Adobe's stock is falling despite an earnings beat
data.description:
By Christine Ji

Adobe's longtime CEO is stepping down, and the lack of acceleration on a key metric signaled that AI isn't driving significant financial benefits yet

Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen will be stepping down from his position.

Adobe's fiscal first-quarter results largely cleared Wall Street's bar, but its modest earnings beat for the period wasn't enough to disprove the narrative around artificial-intelligence disruption that's weighed on software stocks.

One issue is that Adobe's growth slowed on annual recurring revenue, which at $26 billion was up 10.9%. The company posted 11.5% growth on the metric in its fiscal fourth quarter.

"We continue to believe that ARR reacceleration remains the focus for investors to get more constructive," RBC Capital Markets analyst Matthew Swanson said in a note to clients.

Additionally, Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen will be stepping down from the role after 18 years, according to a company press release. He will remain as chair of the board and continue to lead the company until a successor is named.

Those developments overshadowed some of Adobe's financial highlights. First-quarter 2026 revenue was $6.4 billion, growing 12% from a year before. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting $6.3 billion. Adjusted earnings per share came out to $6.06, beating the Wall Street consensus estimate of $5.87.

Shares of Adobe (ADBE) were down more than 7% in after-hours trading on Thursday following the announcement. Adobe's stock has fallen 19% so far this year as the company faces heightened competition from AI-native design and content-creation platforms.

See more: Adobe's stock is at a multiyear low, but the pummeling may not be done yet

The company's fastest-growing segment was Business Professionals & Consumer subscription revenue, which posted a 16% year-over-year increase to $1.78 billion. This category represents Adobe's Document Cloud business, which includes PDF and e-signature tools. Creative & Marketing Professionals subscription revenue - which encompasses Adobe's digital-media software - grew 12% to $4.39 billion.

Total annual recurring revenue exiting the first quarter was $26.1 billion. According to Adobe, "AI-first" ARR more than tripled year over year.

Adobe's forward guidance didn't signal to investors that much of a revenue acceleration is ahead. For the current fiscal second quarter, Adobe guided for revenue between $6.43 billion and $6.48 billion and adjusted earnings per share between $5.80 and $5.85. Wall Street analysts were expecting $6.43 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of $5.68. The company reaffirmed its full-year outlook of $25.9 billion to $26.1 billion in revenue.

Read: 7 software stocks to buy as the sector shows signs of life

-Christine Ji

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: W.P. Carey Raises Quarterly Dividend by 1.1%
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

W.P. Carey is boosting its quarterly dividend by about 1.1% to 93 cents a share, up from 92 cents a share.

The new payout, equal to $3.72 a year, represents a 5.2% annual yield based on the company's Thursday closing price of $71.81.

The dividend will be paid April 15 to shareholders of record as of March 31, the real estate investment trust said Thursday.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Insulet Initiates Voluntary Correction For Omnipod 5, Expects $40M of Related Costs
data.description:
By Amira McKee

Insulet is initiating a voluntary medical device correction for certain lots of its Omnipod 5 pods that may not deliver insulin correctly.

The medical device company said Thursday that some pods may have a small tear in the internal tubing that delivers insulin. As a result, insulin may leak inside the device instead of being fully infused into the body as intended, the company said.

Insulet expects to incur up to $40 million in costs associated with the correction in 2026 and does not anticipate updating its previously issued guidance for the year, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company has received 18 reports of serious adverse events associated with high blood glucose levels, but no deaths have been reported. The issue does not affect continuous glucose monitoring systems or readings.

Insulet continues to manufacture and ship the product, and does not anticipate a disruption to customer shipments, product availability or new patient starts. The pods involved with the correction represent approximately 1.5% of annual Omnipod 5 pod production globally, it said.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has been notified of the correction, the company said.

Write to Amira McKee at amira.mckee@wsj.com

data.title: Insulet recalls some insulin devices in US over leakage risk
data.description:
Insulet has recalled specific batches of its insulin-delivery pods in the U.S. after finding a manufacturing defect that could stop diabetic users from getting their full dose of the blood sugar-regulating hormone, the medical device maker said on Thursday.

Shares of the company fell nearly 7% in extended trading.

The company said a small tear in internal tubing can cause insulin to leak inside the device instead of entering the body.

That can lead to high blood sugar levels, and in severe cases, a dangerous condition where the body starts breaking down fat too quickly that needs urgent medical care, Insulet said.

The affected product, the Omnipod 5, is an automated insulin-delivery system that attaches to the skin and delivers insulin to people with diabetes.

Insulet has received 18 reports of serious adverse events, including hospitalizations. No deaths have been reported.

It said only specific lots are affected and that all other devices in the line remain safe to use.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has been notified of the issue, the company said.

data.title: Afya Limited Announces Dividend Distribution of R$307.4 Million
data.description:
BELO HORIZONTE, Brazil--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 12, 2026--

Afya Limited (; B3: A2FY34) ("Afya" or the "Company"), the leading medical education group and medical practice solutions provider in Brazil, announced that its board of directors (the "Board") approved its second distribution of a cash dividend.

On March 12, 2026, the Company's Board of Directors approved dividend distribution in the amount of R$307.4 million, representing 40% of the Company's consolidated net income for the year ended December 31, 2025 and a dividend per share of R$3.446838, payable in U.S. dollars on April 6, 2026, to the shareholders on record as of the close of business on March 25, 2025. The payment will be made at the exchange rate (PTAX) to be published by the Brazilian Central Bank on March 13, 2026.

"This dividend declaration reflects our disciplined approach to capital allocation and the strength of our capital structure," said Luis Blanco, Afya's CFO. "We also continue to execute our share repurchase program, with approximately 60% of the current authorized buyback program remaining available for execution until December 2026. Combined with dividends, we expect to distribute 50% of the Company's consolidated net income for the year ended 2025, reinforcing our commitment to shareholder returns. We remain confident in our long-term growth and value creation strategy and maintaining our growth guidance".

About Afya Limited (, B3: A2FY34)

Afya is a leading medical education group in Brazil based on the number of medical school seats, delivering an end-to-end physician-centric ecosystem that serves and empowers students and physicians to transform their ambitions into rewarding lifelong experiences from the moment they join us as medical students through their medical residency preparation, graduation program, continuing medical education activities and offering medical practice solutions to help doctors enhance their healthcare services through their whole career.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260312969198/en/

CONTACT: Investor Relations Contact:

Afya Limited

ir@afya.com.br
